headline: Persistent agent memory and multi-model routing platforms anchored the quarter as on-device inference broke into production.
body: The dominant theme was stateful, long-running agents: Hermes shipped persistent memory and skill libraries across sessions, reaching roughly 200K GitHub stars, and Google Cloud detailed event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation for multi-day workflows. Microsoft moved Copilot Cowork to general availability and positioned Azure as a multi-model platform routing across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork, and DeepSeek, formalizing model routing as an enterprise layer. On-device inference advanced sharply with Gemma 4, whose 2B model now matches last year's 27B dense model and runs offline multimodal tasks through the AI Edge Gallery. Anthropic added enterprise-managed MCP authentication and Claude Code artifacts, while breakout techniques including step reduction, student-teacher training, and delegation chains signaled rotation toward training efficiency and agent orchestration.
